mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.5 percent signals deteriorating consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based economic strength, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio for 20-day forward returns over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases yields positive 60-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, extreme monetary accommodation forces investors into equities, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a negative information coefficient over any 36-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.1 million units during a post-shock recovery phase signals weak construction demand and reduced economic momentum, leading to negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exceed zero in more than 65 percent of periods when HOUST falls below 1100 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect stronger manufacturing output and higher corporate revenues, consistently generating positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a statistical significance level above 90 percent over a 5-year sample.
